questions.stem:
—I ______ the book yesterday. It was really interesting.
—Great! How long ______ you ______ it?
questions.explanation: 第一空有明确过去时间状语yesterday，必须用一般过去时bought，排除B和D；第二空问"拥有多久"，是持续到现在的状态，用现在完成时have had，疑问句将have提前。C选项did buy强调动作，与how long（时间段）不搭配。解题技巧：看到yesterday/last week等用过去时；看到how long/for/since用现在完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: This is the museum ______ we visited last summer holiday.
questions.explanation: 先行词museum是物，在定语从句中作visited的宾语，可用which/that引导，也可省略。A选项who指人；C选项where在从句中作地点状语；D选项when作时间状语。从句中visited缺少宾语，故用which。解题技巧：先行词是物，从句缺主语/宾语用which/that；先行词是地点，但从句缺状语才用where。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: Our classroom ______ by the students every afternoon.
questions.explanation: 主语classroom是动作clean的承受者，用被动语态；时间状语every afternoon表示习惯性动作，用一般现在时。A是主动语态；B缺be动词；D是过去时，与every afternoon不符。解题技巧：被动语态结构为be+done，先看主语与动词关系，再看时间状语确定时态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Beijing on business.
questions.explanation: 根据后句"He has gone to Beijing"（他去北京了，不在本地），可知敲门的不可能是他，表否定推测用can't（不可能）。must be表肯定推测"一定是"；might be表不确定"可能是"；can通常不用于肯定推测。解题技巧：根据语境判断是肯定还是否定推测，肯定用must，否定用can't，不确定用may/might。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are not ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: 固定搭配be satisfied with（对……满意）。A和D修饰物；B修饰人，但be excited about表示"对……兴奋"，不符合句意"对分数不满意"。解题技巧：记住常见形容词搭配，-ed形容词修饰人，-ing形容词修饰物。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: un-是形容词前缀，表示否定，加在形容词healthy前。A是名词；C是副词；D是比较级。解题技巧：前缀un-/dis-/im-通常加在形容词前构成反义词，注意词性保持一致。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should take immediate ______ to solve this environmental problem.
questions.explanation: 固定搭配take action（采取行动）。A是动词；C是形容词；D是"活动"，take activity搭配不当。解题技巧：immediate是形容词，后接名词；记住take action这一固定短语。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is a serious problem for our planet. Every year, about 8 million tons of plastic waste end up in the ocean. This causes great harm to sea animals. However, a group of students in our school are trying to make a change.

Last month, the Science Club started a recycling project. They 8 special bins in each classroom to collect plastic bottles. At first, many students didn't pay attention to it. They just threw bottles everywhere 9 they didn't understand the importance of recycling.

The club members didn't give up. They made colorful posters and gave short speeches during break time. They explained 10 plastic pollution was harmful and how recycling could help. Slowly, more students started to use the bins correctly.

By the end of last week, they 11 collected over 500 bottles. These bottles were sent to a local factory 12 they were processed into new products like pencil cases and rulers.
questions.explanation: set up建立，设立。句意：他们在每个教室设立特殊的垃圾桶。A张贴；C编造；D捡起。根据宾语special bins（特殊垃圾桶），应是"设立"。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is a serious problem for our planet. Every year, about 8 million tons of plastic waste end up in the ocean. This causes great harm to sea animals. However, a group of students in our school are trying to make a change.

Last month, the Science Club started a recycling project. They 8 special bins in each classroom to collect plastic bottles. At first, many students didn't pay attention to it. They just threw bottles everywhere 9 they didn't understand the importance of recycling.

The club members didn't give up. They made colorful posters and gave short speeches during break time. They explained 10 plastic pollution was harmful and how recycling could help. Slowly, more students started to use the bins correctly.

By the end of last week, they 11 collected over 500 bottles. These bottles were sent to a local factory 12 they were processed into new products like pencil cases and rulers.
questions.explanation: 前后是因果关系。句意：他们到处扔瓶子，因为他们不理解回收的重要性。B虽然；C如果；D除非。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is a serious problem for our planet. Every year, about 8 million tons of plastic waste end up in the ocean. This causes great harm to sea animals. However, a group of students in our school are trying to make a change.

Last month, the Science Club started a recycling project. They 8 special bins in each classroom to collect plastic bottles. At first, many students didn't pay attention to it. They just threw bottles everywhere 9 they didn't understand the importance of recycling.

The club members didn't give up. They made colorful posters and gave short speeches during break time. They explained 10 plastic pollution was harmful and how recycling could help. Slowly, more students started to use the bins correctly.

By the end of last week, they 11 collected over 500 bottles. These bottles were sent to a local factory 12 they were processed into new products like pencil cases and rulers.
questions.explanation: 宾语从句连接词。根据后文"how recycling could help"（回收如何有帮助）的并列结构，前文应对应"为什么塑料污染有害"。句意：他们解释为什么塑料污染有害以及回收如何有帮助。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is a serious problem for our planet. Every year, about 8 million tons of plastic waste end up in the ocean. This causes great harm to sea animals. However, a group of students in our school are trying to make a change.

Last month, the Science Club started a recycling project. They 8 special bins in each classroom to collect plastic bottles. At first, many students didn't pay attention to it. They just threw bottles everywhere 9 they didn't understand the importance of recycling.

The club members didn't give up. They made colorful posters and gave short speeches during break time. They explained 10 plastic pollution was harmful and how recycling could help. Slowly, more students started to use the bins correctly.

By the end of last week, they 11 collected over 500 bottles. These bottles were sent to a local factory 12 they were processed into new products like pencil cases and rulers.
questions.explanation: 现在完成时标志词。By the end of last week（到上周末为止）是过去时间点，主句用过去完成时或一般过去时，但此处强调"已经收集"，用already。yet用于否定句/疑问句；still表示仍然；ever表示曾经。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is a serious problem for our planet. Every year, about 8 million tons of plastic waste end up in the ocean. This causes great harm to sea animals. However, a group of students in our school are trying to make a change.

Last month, the Science Club started a recycling project. They 8 special bins in each classroom to collect plastic bottles. At first, many students didn't pay attention to it. They just threw bottles everywhere 9 they didn't understand the importance of recycling.

The club members didn't give up. They made colorful posters and gave short speeches during break time. They explained 10 plastic pollution was harmful and how recycling could help. Slowly, more students started to use the bins correctly.

By the end of last week, they 11 collected over 500 bottles. These bottles were sent to a local factory 12 they were processed into new products like pencil cases and rulers.
questions.explanation: 定语从句关系副词。先行词factory是地点，从句"they were processed..."主谓齐全，缺地点状语，用where（=in which）。who指人；which作主语/宾语；when指时间。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Do you find it hard to get up early for school? For 14-year-old Zhang Wei, this was the biggest challenge last semester. He always arrived late and missed the first class. His teachers were worried, and his parents tried everything, but nothing worked.

Everything changed when his father bought him a smart alarm clock. Unlike ordinary clocks, this one has wheels. When the alarm rings, the clock jumps off the table and runs around the room. Zhang Wei has to get up and catch it to turn off the noise. "It's like playing a game every morning," he laughed. "Now I'm never late."

The smart clock was invented by a college student two years ago. It was designed to help heavy sleepers. Besides running away, it can also record personal messages. Zhang Wei's father recorded his voice saying, "Son, time to rise!" This makes the wake-up call more effective.

However, experts remind us not to rely too much on technology. Dr. Li, a sleep scientist, suggests going to bed earlier instead. "Good sleep habits are more important than smart gadgets," she said. Still, for Zhang Wei, the clock has been a lifesaver. He even joined the school's morning running club recently.
questions.stem: What was Zhang Wei's problem last semester?
options: A. He couldn't finish his homework on time.
B. He always arrived late for school.
C. He had trouble sleeping at night.
D. He missed his smart clock every morning.
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第一段"He always arrived late and missed the first class"可知，张伟上学期总是迟到。A选项作业问题未提及；C选项失眠错误，他是起不来；D选项理解错误。解题技巧：细节题要定位原文，找到对应句子，避免主观臆断。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Do you find it hard to get up early for school? For 14-year-old Zhang Wei, this was the biggest challenge last semester. He always arrived late and missed the first class. His teachers were worried, and his parents tried everything, but nothing worked.

Everything changed when his father bought him a smart alarm clock. Unlike ordinary clocks, this one has wheels. When the alarm rings, the clock jumps off the table and runs around the room. Zhang Wei has to get up and catch it to turn off the noise. "It's like playing a game every morning," he laughed. "Now I'm never late."

The smart clock was invented by a college student two years ago. It was designed to help heavy sleepers. Besides running away, it can also record personal messages. Zhang Wei's father recorded his voice saying, "Son, time to rise!" This makes the wake-up call more effective.

However, experts remind us not to rely too much on technology. Dr. Li, a sleep scientist, suggests going to bed earlier instead. "Good sleep habits are more important than smart gadgets," she said. Still, for Zhang Wei, the clock has been a lifesaver. He even joined the school's morning running club recently.
questions.stem: What does the word "gadgets" in Paragraph 4 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文，前文提到smart alarm clock（智能闹钟），此处Dr. Li说"良好的睡眠习惯比智能gadgets更重要"，gadgets指代前文提到的智能设备/电子产品。A睡眠习惯；C晨练；D大学生。解题技巧：通过上下文逻辑和指代关系猜测词义，注意同义词或上下义词替换。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Do you find it hard to get up early for school? For 14-year-old Zhang Wei, this was the biggest challenge last semester. He always arrived late and missed the first class. His teachers were worried, and his parents tried everything, but nothing worked.

Everything changed when his father bought him a smart alarm clock. Unlike ordinary clocks, this one has wheels. When the alarm rings, the clock jumps off the table and runs around the room. Zhang Wei has to get up and catch it to turn off the noise. "It's like playing a game every morning," he laughed. "Now I'm never late."

The smart clock was invented by a college student two years ago. It was designed to help heavy sleepers. Besides running away, it can also record personal messages. Zhang Wei's father recorded his voice saying, "Son, time to rise!" This makes the wake-up call more effective.

However, experts remind us not to rely too much on technology. Dr. Li, a sleep scientist, suggests going to bed earlier instead. "Good sleep habits are more important than smart gadgets," she said. Still, for Zhang Wei, the clock has been a lifesaver. He even joined the school's morning running club recently.
questions.stem: What can we learn from the passage?
options: A. Zhang Wei's father invented the smart clock.
B. The clock can only record voice messages.
C. Zhang Wei has developed better living habits.
D. Dr. Li thinks the smart clock is necessary for everyone.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据最后一句"He even joined the school's morning running club recently"（他甚至加入了晨跑俱乐部），可推断张伟养成了更好的生活习惯。A错误，是大学生发明的；B错误，它还会跑；D错误，Dr. Li认为睡眠习惯更重要。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文但需进一步推断，注意even等语气词暗示的变化。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Do you find it hard to get up early for school? For 14-year-old Zhang Wei, this was the biggest challenge last semester. He always arrived late and missed the first class. His teachers were worried, and his parents tried everything, but nothing worked.

Everything changed when his father bought him a smart alarm clock. Unlike ordinary clocks, this one has wheels. When the alarm rings, the clock jumps off the table and runs around the room. Zhang Wei has to get up and catch it to turn off the noise. "It's like playing a game every morning," he laughed. "Now I'm never late."

The smart clock was invented by a college student two years ago. It was designed to help heavy sleepers. Besides running away, it can also record personal messages. Zhang Wei's father recorded his voice saying, "Son, time to rise!" This makes the wake-up call more effective.

However, experts remind us not to rely too much on technology. Dr. Li, a sleep scientist, suggests going to bed earlier instead. "Good sleep habits are more important than smart gadgets," she said. Still, for Zhang Wei, the clock has been a lifesaver. He even joined the school's morning running club recently.
questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: A. The History of Alarm Clocks
B. How to Sleep Well Every Night
C. A Smart Solution to a Common Problem
D. The Importance of Morning Exercise
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章主要讲述张伟用智能闹钟解决起床困难的问题。A选项范围过大；B选项是专家建议，非重点；D选项是结果而非主题。C选项"一个常见问题的智能解决方案"准确概括全文。解题技巧：标题要概括全文核心内容，既要具体又要全面，避免以偏概全。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
How can we live a greener life? Here are some simple tips that everyone can follow.

First, reduce waste. Bring your own shopping bags instead of using plastic ones. 17 Also, try to buy products with less packaging.

Second, save energy. Turn off the lights when you leave a room. 18 Unplug chargers when you're not using them.

Third, choose green transportation. 19 Walking or riding a bike is good for both your health and the environment. If you have to take a bus, try to use public transportation instead of private cars.

Finally, spread the word. 20 Tell your friends and family why it's important. Together, we can make our planet a better place.
options: A. This can save electricity and reduce your bills.
B. However, don't force others to change immediately.
C. Use a reusable water bottle when you go out.
D. When possible, avoid driving short distances.
E. Talk to others about what you've learned.
questions.explanation: 前文讲减少浪费，自带购物袋，此处应继续举例减少塑料使用。C选项"出门使用可重复使用的水瓶"承接上文，符合reduce waste的主题。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
How can we live a greener life? Here are some simple tips that everyone can follow.

First, reduce waste. Bring your own shopping bags instead of using plastic ones. 17 Also, try to buy products with less packaging.

Second, save energy. Turn off the lights when you leave a room. 18 Unplug chargers when you're not using them.

Third, choose green transportation. 19 Walking or riding a bike is good for both your health and the environment. If you have to take a bus, try to use public transportation instead of private cars.

Finally, spread the word. 20 Tell your friends and family why it's important. Together, we can make our planet a better place.
options: A. This can save electricity and reduce your bills.
B. However, don't force others to change immediately.
C. Use a reusable water bottle when you go out.
D. When possible, avoid driving short distances.
E. Talk to others about what you've learned.
questions.explanation: 本段主题是save energy（节约能源），前文说关灯，后文说拔掉充电器。A选项"这能省电并减少账单"承上启下，解释为什么要这么做。
questions.label: 短文填空

D. When possible, avoid driving short distances.

questions.label: 短文填空

questions.label: 短文填空
